site.title: Nathan Bellow
site.description: Software Engineer based in Montréal - Ruby on Rails, Vue, and full-stack product development.

hero.title: Software Engineer
hero.introduction: I build full-stack products that people actually use: from multi-tenant CRMs serving financial advisors to Book reading platforms reaching 2,000+ readers.

skills.title: Technical Skills
skills.intro: Tools and practices I reach for when shipping reliable software.

experience.title: Experience
experience.intro: Where I've shipped code, led teams, and moved metrics.

cocooning.h1: Building a multi-tenant CRM for financial advisors (Ruby on Rails / MySQL) - client portfolios, insurance, succession planning, and compliance in one system.
cocooning.h2: Designed a compliance-aware case management module linking tasks, documents, and audit trails - increasing weekly active sessions by 12%.
cocooning.h3: Optimized slow queries and added Redis caching on high-traffic endpoints, cutting average API response times by 30%.

illuxi.h1: Full-stack on illuxiLMS, a bilingual LMS serving 200,000+ learners/month for government, school boards, and enterprise clients (TD Bank, City of Longueuil).
illuxi.h2: Shipped platform features, DevOps workflows (CI/CD, AWS), and bug fixes across the LMS and an internal FinTech app.
illuxi.h3: Promoted to Team Lead: sprint planning, PR review pipeline, cross-team coordination - improving sprint velocity by 40%.
illuxi.h4: Led stakeholder syncs for scoping and triage, raising post-release NPS satisfaction scores by 25%.

agency.h1: Contributed to an app similar to candidate.fyi hiring-workflow SaaS - API integrations and front-end components in a pair-programming environment.

bellow.h1: Built full-stack apps (Vue 3 + Rails) for a Canadian NGO and a school - donation flow redesign increased online donations by 11%.
bellow.h2: Delivered WordPress and Vue 3 static sites on compressed timelines for non-technical stakeholders.

projects.title: Projects
projects.emptyDescription: I'm building new projects to showcase here. Check back soon ; or browse my GitHub in the meantime.
